last_item: Band-1: login brute-force lockout — proved the throttle end-to-end through the HTTP login handler (closes the last band-1 item; band 1 security complete)
last_result: done — the lockout itself (auth.LockoutPolicy default 5/15min/15min, recordFailure, sliding Window, unlock-after-LockFor, success resets) was already fully built + unit-tested in internal/auth. The untested gap was the HTTP wiring: handlers_auth.go claims 'Lockout returns CodeLocked (HTTP 429)' but no test exercised the login handler at the boundary. Added TestLoginLockoutThrottlesAfterMaxAttempts (server_test.go): builds a server with a tight 3-attempt policy, drives real POST /api/auth/login through srv.Handler(), asserts N-1 wrong → 401 CodeAuth, Nth wrong → 429 CodeLocked, then the CORRECT password is ALSO 429 (proves lockout is checked before password verification — no bypass by finally guessing right), and GET /api/auth/status surfaces locked:true + a future locked_until. Time assertion compares deadline to a pre-request timestamp (15-min margin, no wall-clock flake). Test-only change; full gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build). Reviewed (no correctness issues; adopted the reviewer's de-flake suggestion).
notes: Band 1 (security) is now COMPLETE. Next band is 1.5 (data durability): (1) surface 'last good backup was N ago' on Dashboard+Backups (backend field + UI); (2) loud alert when a scheduled backup fails or hasn't succeeded within its window; (3) nudge backups off-host — S3 as recommended default, warn clearly when only local backups exist; (4) restore-test surfacing. Backups plumbing: see internal/backup (objectstore.go), internal/store/backup.go, scheduler package (already has tests). Test conventions: server tests build over store.Open(":memory:") + auth.New + newServer(...); see newTestServer. Integration tests are //go:build integration, skip unless INDIEPG_TEST_SOCKET set, NOT in the normal gate. go/psql/pg_ctl are snap-sandboxed (run with sandbox disabled). TMPDIR is empty in this shell — use /tmp explicitly. Auth lockout: policy/recordFailure in internal/auth/{lockout,authenticator}.go; HTTP handler maps CodeLocked→429 in respond.go.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
